__init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n47\t        self._json_data = json_data\n48\t        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n49\t        self.status_code = status_code\n50\t\n51\t    def json(self):\n52\t        if self._json_data is None:\n53\t            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n54\t        return self._json_data\n55\t\n56\t    def raise_for_status(self):\n57\t        if self.status_code >= 400:\n58\t            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n59\t                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n60\t                request=MagicMock(),\n61\t                response=self,\n62\t            )\n63\t\n64\t\n65\tclass FakeAsyncClient:\n66\t    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n67\t\n68\t    def __init__(self, responder):\n69\t        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n70\t        self._responder = responder\n71\t        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n72\t\n73\t    async def __aenter__(self):\n74\t        return self\n75\t\n76\t    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n77\t        return False\n78\t\n79\t    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n80\t        self.requests.append((url, params))\n81\t        if callable(self._responder):\n82\t            return self._responder(url, params)\n83\t        return self._responder\n84\t\n85\t\n86\t@pytest.fixture\n87\tdef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n88\t    \"\"\"\n89\t    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n90\t    that constructs one gets `responder`. source-type executor unit tests (bead adc-1mzt).\n\nWhere tests/test_fetch_strand.py (bead adc-mwrx) stubs *every* source executor\nto test dispatch/parallelism/coverage, this suite exercises the **real executor\nimplementations** in src/fetch/orchestrator.py against a mocked HTTP layer —\nproving that each fetch source type (kubectl API, ArgoCD API, pod logs, events)\nparses its real response shape into the structured dict synthesize consumes.\n\nhttpx is faked, so no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, or live cluster is hit.\nNo subprocess or filesystem source is touched here (those are covered by the\nlocal-repo integration test at the end).\n\nWhat this suite locks down (the \"various fetch source types execute correctly\"\ncontract):\n\n1. **HTTP source types parse real payloads** — kubectl_pods, kubectl_deployments,\n   kubectl_workflows, argocd_app, logs, and events each turn a realistic API\n   response into the field set the component library expects.\n2. **Missing-argument guards** — every executor returns a structured error dict\n   (and makes no HTTP call) when its required context fields are absent, rather\n   than raising.\n3. **HTTP error handling** — 404s and empty-result envelopes become error dicts,\n   not exceptions (synthesize surfaces caveats, never crashes).\n4. **End-to-end strand wiring** — a real FetchStrand.fetch() over a STATUS\n   utterance, with httpx mocked and local sources stubbed, yields parsed data\n   for each HTTP source — i.e. the strand correctly maps source types to\n   executors to structured results.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock\n\nimport httpx\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.fetch import orchestrator\nfrom src.fetch.commands import FetchContext, FetchRequest, FetchSource, IntentType\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchStrand\n\n# --- httpx fakes -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FakeResponse:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for httpx.Response — serves canned JSON/text + status.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n        self._json_data = json_data\n        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n        self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n                response=self,\n            )\n\n\nclass FakeAsyncClient:\n    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, responder):\n        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n        self._responder = responder\n        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n        return False\n\n    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n        self.requests.append((url, params))\n        if callable(self._responder):\n            return self._responder(url, params)\n        return self._responder\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"\n    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n    that constructs one gets `responder`. Returns the installed client so tests\n    can assert on which URLs/params were hit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def install(responder) -> FakeAsyncClient:\n        client = FakeAsyncClient(responder)\n        # Executors construct via `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=..., verify=...)`;\n        # ignore those kwargs and hand back our instrumented client.\n        monkeypatch.setattr(orchestrator.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", lambda *a, **kw: client)\n        return client\n\n    return install\n\n\ndef _ctx(**kwargs) -> FetchContext:\n    \"\"\"FetchContext with sane kubectl-proxy defaults + per-test overrides.\"\"\"\n    defaults = {\n        \"proxy\": \"http://traefik-test:8001\",\n        \"namespace\": \"myns\",\n        \"project_slug\": \"my-proj\",\n    }\n    defaults.update(kwargs)\n    return FetchContext(**defaults)\n\n\n# --- 1. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1ac9c7be53374f5690a16512","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tFetch strand execution unit tests (bead adc-mwrx).\n3\t\n4\tHermetic, network-free tests for src/fetch/orchestrator.py — the FetchStrand\n5\tthat runs every fetch source for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n6\tresults via a callback, and tracks coverage (succeeded / timed_out / failed).\n7\t\n8\tEvery source executor is stubbed by swapping FetchStrand._source_executors,\n9\tso no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, git repo, or filesystem is touched.\n10\tThe four things this suite locks down:\n11\t\n12\t1. **Per-intent invocation** — for each intent type in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX,\n13\t   the strand invokes exactly that intent's source set (no more, no fewer).\n14\t2. **Parallel execution** — sources within a strand run concurrently, and\n15\t   multiple independent strands run concurrently with each other.\n16\t3. **Streaming** — the on_partial_result callback fires once per source, in\n17\t   completion order, for successes *and* failures.\n18\t4. **Coverage tracking** — succeeded/timed_out/failed are bucketed correctly,\n19\t   success_rate is right, and the right caveats (required vs optional, failed\n20\t   vs timed-out) are attached to FetchResult.\n21\t\"\"\"\n22\t\n23\timport asyncio\n24\timport time\n25\tfrom typing import Any, Callable\n26\t\n27\timport pytest\n28\t\n29\tfrom src.fetch.commands import (\n30\t    FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX,\n31\t    FetchCommandSpec,\n32\t    FetchContext,\n33\t    FetchRequest,\n34\t    FetchSource,\n35\t    IntentType,\n36\t    SourceResult,\n37\t)\n38\tfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchOrchestrator, FetchStrand\n39\t\n40\t# --- executor stubs --------------------------------------------------------\n41\t# Each returns an async fn(context) -> dict matching the executor contract.\n42\t# Stubbing the executor (not the whole strand) means _execute_source's\n43\t# success/timeout/error triage still runs against real SourceResult objects.\n44\t\n45\t\n46\tdef _success_executor(data: dict | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n47\t    \"\"\"Executor that returns `data` (default {\"ok\": True}).\"\"\"\n48\t    payload = data if data is not None else {\"ok\": True}\n49\t\n50\t    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n51\t        return payload\n52\t\n53\t    return _fn\n54\t\n55\t\n56\tdef _slow_executor(delay: float, data: dict | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n57\t    \"\"\"Executor that sleeps `delay` seconds before returning — for timing tests.\"\"\"\n58\t    payload = data if data is not None else {\"ok\": True}\n59\t\n60\t    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n61\t        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n62\t        return payload\n63\t\n64\t    return _fn\n65\t\n66\t\n67\tdef _timeout_executor() -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n68\t    \"\"\"Executor that raises asyncio.TimeoutError — exercises the inner timeout path.\"\"\"\n69\t\n70\t    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n71\t        raise asyncio.TimeoutError()\n72\t\n73\t    return _fn\n74\t\n75\t\n76\tdef _error_executor(exc: Exception | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n77\t    \"\"\"Executor that raises — exercises the error path.\"\"\"\n78\t    error = exc or RuntimeError(\"boom from stub\")\n79\t\n80\t    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n81\t        raise error\n82\t\n83\t    return _fn\n84\t\n85\t\n86\tdef _build_strand(\n87\t    per_source: dict[FetchSource, Callable[[FetchContext], Any]] | None = None,\n88\t) -> tuple[FetchStrand, dict[FetchSource, int]]:\n89\t    \"\"\"\n90\t    Build a FetchStrand whose executors are all stubbed, plus a recorder that\n91\t    counts how many times each source's executor was actually called.\n92\t\n93\t    Sources in `per_source` use the given behavior; every other source falls\n94\t    back to a success stub (so no source is ever left without an executor).\n95\t    The recorder lets tests assert *which* sources were invoked per intent.\n96\t    \"\"\"\n97\t    per_source = per_source or {}\n98\t    strand = FetchStrand()\n99\t    recorder: dict[FetchSource, int] = {}\n100\t\n101\t    def _wrap(src: FetchSource, fn: Callable[[FetchContext], Any]):\n102\t        async def _wrapped(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n103\t            recorder[src] = recorder.get(src, 0) + 1\n104\t            return await fn(ctx)\n105\t\n106\t        return _wrapped\n107\t\n108\t    instrumented = {\n109\t        src: _wrap(src, per_source.get(src, _success_executor())) for src in FetchSource\n110\t    }\n111\t    strand._source_executors = instrumented\n112\t    return strand, recorder\n113\t\n114\t\n115\tdef _request(intent_type: IntentType, **ctx_kwargs) -> FetchRequest:\n116\t    \"\"\"Build a FetchRequest with a throwaway context for the given intent.\"\"\"\n117\t    return FetchRequest(\n118\t        intent_type=intent_type,\n119\t        context=FetchContext(**ctx_kwargs),\n120\t        intent_id=f\"intent-{intent_type.value}-test\",\n121\t        session_id=\"session-test\",\n122\t    )\n123\t\n124\t\n125\t# --- 1. per-intent invocation ---------------------------------------------\n126\t\n127\t\n128\tclass TestPerIntentInvocation:\n129\t @pytest.mark.asyncio\n133\t    async def test_invokes_exactly_its_matrix_sources(self, intent_type):\n134\t        expected = {spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[intent_type]}\n135\t\n136\t each other.\"\"\"\n180\t\n181\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n182\t    async def test_sources_run_concurrently_within_a_strand(self):\n183\t        # STATUS has 7 sources. Each sleeps `delay`; serial would be 7*delay,\n184\t        # concurrent should be ~delay.\n185\t        delay = 0.2\n186\t        n = len(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS])\n187\t        assert n >= 3  # sanity for the concurrency claim\n188\t\n189\t        slow = {src: _slow_executor(delay) for src in FetchSource}\n190\t        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=slow)\n191\t\n192\t        start = time.monotonic()\n193\t        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.STATUS))\n194\t        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start\n195\t\n196\t        # Concurrent: well under the serial bound, at least one source's duration.\n197\t        assert elapsed < n * delay / 2, f\"sources ran serially: {elapsed:.3f}s >= {n * delay / 2}s\"\n198\t        assert elapsed >= delay\n199\t        assert result.coverage.success_rate == 1.0\n200\t\n201\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n202\t    async def test_multiple_strands_execute_in_parallel(self):\n203\t        \"\"\"Four independent fetch() calls finish in ~one wave, not four back-to-back.\"\"\"\n204\t        delay = 0.25\n205\t        slow = {src: _slow_executor(delay) for src in FetchSource}\n206\t\n207\t        async def run_one():\n208\t            strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=slow)\n209\t            return await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.STATUS))\n210\t\n211\t        start = time.monotonic()\n212\t        results = await asyncio.gather(*(run_one() for _ in range(4)))\n213\t        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start\n214\t\n215\t        # 4 serial waves would be ~4*delay; concurrent is ~delay.\n216\t        assert elapsed < delay * 2, f\"strands ran serially: {elapsed:.3f}s\"\n217\t        assert all(r.coverage.success_rate == 1.0 for r in results)\n218\t        assert len(results) in completion order, for every outcome.\"\"\"\n226\t\n227\t    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n228\t    async def test_callback_fires_once_per_source(self):\n229\t        strand, _ = _build_strand()\n230\t        expected = {spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.ACTION]}\n231\t\n232\t        partials: list[tuple[FetchSource, SourceResult]] = []\n233\t        result = await strand.fetch(\n234\t            _request(IntentType.ACTION),\n235\t            on_partial_result=lambda src, res: partials.append((src, res)),\n236\t        )\n237\t\n238\t        assert len(partials) == len(expected)\n239\t        assert {src for src, _ in partials} == expected\n240\t        # Each partial carries a fully-formed SourceResult bound to its source.\n241\t        for src, res in partials:\n242\t            assert isinstance(res, SourceResult)\n243\t            assert res.source == src\n244\t            assert res.status == \"success\"\n245\t        # Final result is )\n375\t        monkeypatch.setattr(\n376\t            \"src.fetch.orchestrator.get_required_sources\",\n377\t            lambda _intent: [FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS],\n378\t        )\n379\t\n380\t assert len(partials) == len(expected)\n467\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"161e2cae-4066-431a-9f1b-ca762db343c9","uuid":"afbd3ae6-8fab-4fbf-ba05-002d0658f666","timestamp":"2026-07-23T19:18:16.003Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/tests/test_fetch_strand.py","content":"\"\"\"\nFetch strand execution unit tests (bead adc-mwrx).\n\nHermetic, network-free tests for src/fetch/orchestrator.py — the FetchStrand\nthat runs every fetch source for an intent concurrently, streams partial\nresults via a callback, and tracks coverage (succeeded / timed_out / failed).\n\nEvery source executor is stubbed by swapping FetchStrand._source_executors,\nso no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, git repo, or filesystem is touched.\nThe four things this suite locks down:\n\n1. **Per-intent invocation** — for each intent type in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX,\n   the strand invokes exactly that intent's source set (no more, no fewer).\n2. **Parallel execution** — sources within a strand run concurrently, and\n   multiple independent strands run concurrently with each other.\n3. **Streaming** — the on_partial_result callback fires once per source, in\n   completion order, for successes *and* failures.\n4. **Coverage tracking** — succeeded/timed_out/failed are bucketed correctly,\n   success_rate is right, and the right caveats (required vs optional, failed\n   vs timed-out) are attached to FetchResult.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.fetch.commands import (\n    FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX,\n    FetchCommandSpec,\n    FetchContext,\n    FetchRequest,\n    FetchSource,\n    IntentType,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchOrchestrator, FetchStrand\n\n# --- executor stubs --------------------------------------------------------\n# Each returns an async fn(context) -> dict matching the executor contract.\n# Stubbing the executor (not the whole strand) means _execute_source's\n# success/timeout/error triage still runs against real SourceResult objects.\n\n\ndef _success_executor(data: dict | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n    \"\"\"Executor that returns `data` (default {\"ok\": True}).\"\"\"\n    payload = data if data is not None else {\"ok\": True}\n\n    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        return payload\n\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _slow_executor(delay: float, data: dict | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n    \"\"\"Executor that sleeps `delay` seconds before returning — for timing tests.\"\"\"\n    payload = data if data is not None else {\"ok\": True}\n\n    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        await asyncio.sleep(delay)\n        return payload\n\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _timeout_executor() -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n    \"\"\"Executor that raises asyncio.TimeoutError — exercises the inner timeout path.\"\"\"\n\n    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        raise asyncio.TimeoutError()\n\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _error_executor(exc: Exception | None = None) -> Callable[[FetchContext], Any]:\n    \"\"\"Executor that raises — exercises the error path.\"\"\"\n    error = exc or RuntimeError(\"boom from stub\")\n\n    async def _fn(_ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        raise error\n\n    return _fn\n\n\ndef _build_strand(\n    per_source: dict[FetchSource, Callable[[FetchContext], Any]] | None = None,\n) -> tuple[FetchStrand, dict[FetchSource, int]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build a FetchStrand whose executors are all stubbed, plus a recorder that\n    counts how many times each source's executor was actually called.\n\n    Sources in `per_source` use the given behavior; every other source falls\n    back to a success stub (so no source is ever left without an executor).\n    The recorder lets tests assert *which* sources were invoked per intent.\n    \"\"\"\n    per_source = per_source or {}\n    strand = FetchStrand()\n    recorder: dict[FetchSource, int] = {}\n\n    def _wrap(src: FetchSource, fn: Callable[[FetchContext], Any]):\n        async def _wrapped(ctx: FetchContext) -> dict:\n            recorder[src] = recorder.get(src, 0) + 1\n            return await fn(ctx)\n\n        return _wrapped\n\n    instrumented = {\n        src: _wrap(src, per_source.get(src, _success_executor())) for src in FetchSource\n    }\n    strand._source_executors = instrumented\n    return strand, recorder\n\n\ndef _request(intent_type: IntentType, **ctx_kwargs) -> FetchRequest:\n    \"\"\"Build a FetchRequest with a throwaway context for the given intent.\"\"\"\n    return FetchRequest(\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        context=FetchContext(**ctx_kwargs),\n        intent_id=f\"intent-{intent_type.value}-test\",\n        session_id=\"session-test\",\n    )\n\n\n# --- 1. per-intent invocation ---------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestPerIntentInvocation:\n    \"\"\"Each intent type must invoke exactly its matrix-defined source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX))\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_invokes_exactly_its_matrix_sources(self, intent_type):\n        expected = {spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[intent_type]}\n\n        strand, recorder = _build_strand()\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(intent_type))\n\n        invoked = {src for src, n in recorder.items() if n > 0}\n        assert invoked == expected, (\n            f\"{intent_type.value}: invoked {invoked}, matrix defines {expected}\"\n        )\n        # Each expected source ran exactly once.\n        for src in expected:\n            assert recorder[src] == 1\n        # No off-matrix source was touched.\n        for src, n in recorder.items():\n            if n > 0:\n                assert src in expected\n        # Result covers the whole matrix set.\n        assert set(result.sources) == expected\n        assert result.coverage.total_sources == len(expected)\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_reminder_intent_invokes_single_source(self):\n        \"\"\"REMINDER is the minimal case — exactly one (required) source.\"\"\"\n        strand, recorder = _build_strand()\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.REMINDER))\n\n        assert {src for src, n in recorder.items() if n > 0} == {FetchSource.REMINDERS}\n        assert result.coverage.total_sources == 1\n        assert result.coverage.success_rate == 1.0\n\n    def test_every_matrix_source_has_a_registered_executor(self):\n        \"\"\"Guard: adding a source to the matrix without an executor would 404 at runtime.\"\"\"\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n        registered = set(strand._source_executors)\n        for intent_type, specs in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX.items():\n            for spec in specs:\n                assert spec.source in registered, (\n                    f\"{spec.source.value} (intent {intent_type.value}) has no executor\"\n                )\n\n\n# --- 2. parallel execution -------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestParallelExecution:\n    \"\"\"Sources run concurrently within a strand; strands run concurrently with each other.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_sources_run_concurrently_within_a_strand(self):\n        # STATUS has 7 sources. Each sleeps `delay`; serial would be 7*delay,\n        # concurrent should be ~delay.\n        delay = 0.2\n        n = len(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS])\n        assert n >= 3  # sanity for the concurrency claim\n\n        slow = {src: _slow_executor(delay) for src in FetchSource}\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=slow)\n\n        start = time.monotonic()\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.STATUS))\n        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start\n\n        # Concurrent: well under the serial bound, at least one source's duration.\n        assert elapsed < n * delay / 2, f\"sources ran serially: {elapsed:.3f}s >= {n * delay / 2}s\"\n        assert elapsed >= delay\n        assert result.coverage.success_rate == 1.0\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_multiple_strands_execute_in_parallel(self):\n        \"\"\"Four independent fetch() calls finish in ~one wave, not four back-to-back.\"\"\"\n        delay = 0.25\n        slow = {src: _slow_executor(delay) for src in FetchSource}\n\n        async def run_one():\n            strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=slow)\n            return await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.STATUS))\n\n        start = time.monotonic()\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(run_one() for _ in range(4)))\n        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start\n\n        # 4 serial waves would be ~4*delay; concurrent is ~delay.\n        assert elapsed < delay * 2, f\"strands ran serially: {elapsed:.3f}s\"\n        assert all(r.coverage.success_rate == 1.0 for r in results)\n        assert len(results) == 4\n\n\n# --- 3. streaming ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestStreaming:\n    \"\"\"on_partial_result fires once per source, in completion order, for every outcome.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_callback_fires_once_per_source(self):\n        strand, _ = _build_strand()\n        expected = {spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.ACTION]}\n\n        partials: list[tuple[FetchSource, SourceResult]] = []\n        result = await strand.fetch(\n            _request(IntentType.ACTION),\n            on_partial_result=lambda src, res: partials.append((src, res)),\n        )\n\n        assert len(partials) == len(expected)\n        assert {src for src, _ in partials} == expected\n        # Each partial carries a fully-formed SourceResult bound to its source.\n        for src, res in partials:\n            assert isinstance(res, SourceResult)\n            assert res.source == src\n            assert res.status == \"success\"\n        # Final result is consistent with the streamed partials.\n        assert set(result.sources) == expected\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_callback_fires_for_failed_and_timed_out_sources(self):\n        \"\"\"Streaming must not silently drop non-success outcomes.\"\"\"\n        behavior = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: _error_executor(ValueError(\"pod boom\")),\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: _timeout_executor(),\n        }\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=behavior)\n        request = _request(IntentType.ACTION)  # both sources above are in ACTION\n\n        partials: list[tuple[FetchSource, str]] = []\n        await strand.fetch(request, on_partial_result=lambda s, r: partials.append((s, r.status)))\n\n        n = len(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.ACTION])\n        assert len(partials) == n  # one partial per source regardless of outcome\n        statuses = dict(partials)\n        assert statuses[FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS] == \"error\"\n        assert statuses[FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP] == \"timeout\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_no_callback_does_not_raise(self):\n        \"\"\"Omitting on_partial_result is the normal path — must be a no-op.\"\"\"\n        strand, _ = _build_strand()\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.LOOKUP))\n        assert result.coverage.success_rate == 1.0\n\n\n# --- 4. coverage tracking --------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestCoverageTracking:\n    \"\"\"succeeded / timed_out / failed buckets, success_rate, and caveats.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_all_success(self):\n        strand, _ = _build_strand()\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.BRAINSTORM))\n        cov = result.coverage\n        n = len(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.BRAINSTORM])\n\n        assert cov.total_sources == n\n        assert set(cov.succeeded) == {\n            spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.BRAINSTORM]\n        }\n        assert cov.timed_out == []\n        assert cov.failed == []\n        assert cov.skipped == []\n        assert cov.success_rate == 1.0\n        assert cov.has_required_failure is False\n        assert result.caveats is None  # nothing went wrong\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_timeouts_bucketed_with_caveat(self):\n        behavior = {src: _timeout_executor() for src in FetchSource}\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=behavior)\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.LOOKUP))\n\n        expected = {spec.source for spec in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.LOOKUP]}\n        assert set(result.coverage.timed_out) == expected\n        assert result.coverage.succeeded == []\n        assert result.coverage.failed == []\n        assert result.coverage.success_rate == 0.0\n        # Every timed-out source produces a caveat.\n        assert result.caveats is not None\n        assert len(result.caveats) == len(expected)\n        for src in expected:\n            assert any(src.value in c and \"timed out\" in c for c in result.caveats)\n            assert result.sources[src].status == \"timeout\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_required_failure_emits_required_caveat(self):\n        \"\"\"KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS is required for ACTION — its failure is flagged required.\"\"\"\n        behavior = {FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: _error_executor(ValueError(\"deploy down\"))}\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=behavior)\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.ACTION))\n\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in result.coverage.failed\n        assert result.coverage.has_required_failure is True\n        assert _error_executor(ValueError(\"bead cli missing\"))}\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=behavior)\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.ACTION))\n\n        assert  }\n        strand, _ = _build_strand(per_source=behavior)\n        result = await strand.fetch(_request(IntentType.STATUS))\n\n        n = len(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX[IntentType.STATUS])\n        assert assert result.coverage.success_rate == pytest.approx((n - 2) / n)\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_real_per_task_timeout_via_wait_for(self, monkeypatch):\n        \"\"\"\n not-found —\n372\t        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". pod_data.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n828\t        if not container_statuses:\n829\t            return \"0/0\"\n830\t\n831\t        ready_count = sum(1 for cs in container_statuses if cs.get(\"ready\", False))\n832\t        total_count = len(container_statuses)\n833\t\n834\t        return f\"{ready_count}/{total_count}\"\n835\t\n836\t\n837\tclass FetchOrchestrator:\n838\t    \"\"\"\n839\t    Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n840\t\n841\t    This is a convenience wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n842\t    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n843\t\n844\t    Features:\n845\t    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n846\t    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n847\t    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n848\t    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n849\t    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n850\t    \"\"\"\n851\t\n852\t    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n853\t        \"\"\"\n854\t        Initialize the orchestrator.\n855\t\n856\t        Args:\n857\t            fetch_strand: FetchStrand instance (uses global if not provided)\n858\t        \"\"\"\n859\t        if fetch_strand is None:\n860\t            self._strand = FetchStrand()\n861\t        else:\n862\t            self._strand = fetch_strand\n863\t\n864\t    async def execute_fetch(\n865\t        self,\n866\t        request: FetchRequest,\n867\t        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n868\t    ) -> FetchResult:\n869\t        \"\"\"\n870\t        Execute a fetch request with concurrent source execution.\n871\t\n872\t        Args:\n873\t            request: The fetch request with intent type and context\n874\t            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n875\t\n876\t        Returns:\n877\t            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n878\t        \"\"\"\n879\t        logger.info(\n880\t            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n881\t            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n882\t        )\n883\t\n884\t        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n885\t        # - Concurrent execution\n886\t        # - Per-source timeouts\n887\t        # - Streaming callbacks\n888\t        # - Coverage tracking\n889\t        result = await self._strand.fetch(\n890\t            request=request,\n891\t            on_partial_result=stream_callback,\n892\t        )\n893\t\n894\t        logger.info(\n895\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n896\t            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n897\t orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, AsyncIterator, Callable, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom .commands import (\n    FetchContext,\n    FetchCoverage,\n    FetchRequest,\n    FetchResult,\n    FetchSource,\n    IntentType,\n    KUBECTL_PROXIES,\n    get_fetch_commands,\n    get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom .clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, resolve_argocd_endpoint\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS: self._fetch_bead_details,\n            FetchSource.CI_STATUS: self._fetch_ci_status,\n            FetchSource.COMPONENTS: self._fetch_components,\n            FetchSource.LOGS: self._fetch_logs,\n            FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n            FetchSource.REMINDERS: self._fetch_reminders,\n            FetchSource.FS_EXPLORE: self._fetch_fs_explore,\n            FetchSource.FS_README: self._fetch_fs_readme,\n            FetchSource.FS_HOME: self._fetch_fs_home,\n        }\n\n    async def fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"error\": \"Namespace not found\",\n                    \"pods\": [],\n                }\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"pods\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_deployments(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch deployment status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n \"\"),\n                    })\n\n                workflows.sort(key=lambda x: x.get(\"started_at\", \"\"), reverse=True)\n\n                return {\n                    \"project\": project_slug,\n                    \"workflows\": workflows[:10],\n                    \"count\": len(workflows),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"workflows\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\n\n        The ArgoCD endpoint is resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n\n        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n        \"\"\"\n        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n        if not app_name:\n            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n\n        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n                cluster=context.cluster,\n            )\n\n        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n\n                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch bead list from the repo's .beads/ workspace (local or remote).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        project_slug = context.project_slug\n\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved for this project\"}\n\n        try:\n            if context.ssh_target:\n                # Remote: check for .beads and run bf via SSH in one shot\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target,\n                    f\"cd {repo_path} && [ -f .beads/issues.jsonl ] && bf list --status=open --limit=50 2>&1 || echo '{{\\\"error\\\":\\\"no beads workspace\\\"}}'\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n            else:\n                from pathlib import Path as _Path\n                if not (_Path(repo_path) / \".beads\" / \"issues.jsonl\").exists():\n                    return {\"error\": f\"No .beads workspace at {repo_path}\"}\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"bf\", \"list\", \"--status=open\", \"--limit=50\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    cwd=repo_path,\n                )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode == 0:\n                import json as _json\n                try:\n                    beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                except Exception:\n                    beads = []\n                return {\n                    \"project\": project_slug,\n                    \"repo\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                }\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode().strip() or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return {\"error\": \"bf CLI not found in PATH\"}\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_details(self,     \"project\": context.project_slug,\n            \"components\": [],\n            \"count\": 0,\n        }\n\n    async def _fetch_logs(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch  \"\"\"Fetch events from a namespace.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            return {\"error\": \"No namespace This module maps each cluster to\n6\tits ArgoCD API base URL and an `access` mode, loaded from\n7\t``config/clusters.yaml`` and hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache — the same\n8\tstrategy every hot-reloaded config artifact uses (stat the file each call,\n9\tre-read only when its mtime changed).\n10\t\n11\tThe fetch strand holds no ArgoCD credentials, so it can only *consume*\n12\t``read-only-proxy`` endpoints. Any cluster that is unmapped, or mapped to an\n13\taccess mode the strand cannot satisfy (``authenticated`` with no credential\n14\tpath), resolves as **unsatisfiable** — the ArgoCD source then fails with an\n15\thonest ``fetch_coverage`` caveat. Preserve the previously-known-good cache rather than poisoning it:\n54\t        # return whatever is cached (possibly empty) so a malformed edit does\n55\t        # not flip every mapped cluster to unmapped mid-flight.\n56\t        return _cache or {}\n57\t    if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n58\t        logger.error(f\"clusters config {path} is not a mapping (got {type(raw).__name__})\")\n59\t        return _cache or {}\n60\t    clusters = raw.get(\"clusters\", {})\n61\t    if not isinstance(clusters, dict):\n62\t        logger.error(f\"'clusters' in {path} is not a mapping (got {type(clusters).__name__})\")\n63\t        return _cache or {}\n64\t    return clusters\n65\t\n66\t\n67\tdef get_clusters(force: bool = False) -> dict:\n68\t    \"\"\"\n69\t    Return the parsed ``clusters`` mapping, reloading only when the file's\n70\t    mtime has changed (or when ``force`` is set, for tests).\n71\t\n72\t    A benign parse failure returns the last good cache instead of an empty\n73\t    dict, so a transiently-malformed edit never silently unmaps everything.\n74\t    \"\"\"\n75\t    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n76\t    try:\n77\t        mtime = CLUSTERS_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n78\t    except OSError:\n79\t        # File vanished: keep the last-known-good cache (best effort).\n80\t        return _cache or {}\n81\t\n82\t    if force or _cache is None or mtime != _cache_mtime:\n83\t        parsed = _read_clusters_file()\n84\t        # Don't poison the cache on a parse failure: _read_clusters_file\n85\t        # already returns the prior cache in that case, so this is a no-op\n86\t        # when parsing failed — but guard explicitly for clarity.\n87\t        _cache = parsed\n88\t        _cache_mtime = mtime\n89\t        logger.debug(f\"Reloaded clusters config ({len(parsed)} clusters) from {CLUSTERS_PATH}\")\n90\t    return _cache\n91\t\n92\t\n93\tdef reset_cache() -> None:\n94\t    \"\"\"Clear the mtime cache (test hook).\"\"\"\n95\t    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n96\t    _cache = None\n97\t    _cache_mtime = None\n98\t\n99\t\n100\t@dataclass(frozen=True)\n101\tclass ArgocdResolution:\n102\t    \"\"\"Result of resolving a cluster to an ArgoCD endpoint.\n103\t\n104\t    ``satisfiable`` is True only when the strand can actually issue the query —\n105\t    i.e. the cluster is mapped AND its access mode is consumable without\n106\t    credentials. When False, ``reason`` explains why (for the caveat strip).\n107\t    \"\"\"\n108\t\n109\t    cluster: Optional[str]\n110\t    argocd_api: Optional[str]\n111\t    access: Optional[str]\n112\t    satisfiable: bool\n113\t    reason: Optional[str] = None\n114\t\n115\t\n116\tdef resolve_argocd_endpoint(cluster: Optional[str]) -> ArgocdResolution:\n117\t    \"\"\"\n118\t    Resolve a cluster name to a consumable ArgoCD API base URL.\n119\t\n120\t    Resolution rules (every unsatisfiable case carries a human-readable\n121\t    ``reason`` for the fetch_coverage caveat):\n122\t\n123\t    * ``cluster`` is None/empty → unsatisfiable (\"no cluster configured …\")\n124\t    * cluster absent from clusters.yaml → unsatisfiable (\"no ArgoCD mapping …\")\n125\t    * access == ``read-only-proxy`` → satisfiable, argocd_api set\n126\t    * access == ``authenticated`` → unsatisfiable (\"requires authentication …\")\n127\t    * any other/unknown access mode → unsatisfiable (\"unsupported access mode …\")\n128\t    \"\"\"\n129\t    if not cluster:\n130\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n131\t            cluster=None,\n132\t            argocd_api=None,\n133\t            access=None,\n134\t            satisfiable=False,\n135\t            reason=\"No cluster configured for this project; ArgoCD endpoint cannot be resolved\",\n136\t        )\n137\t\n138\t    entry = get_clusters().get(cluster)\n139\t    if entry is None:\n140\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n141\t            cluster=cluster,\n142\t            argocd_api=None,\n143\t            access=None,\n144\t            satisfiable=False,\n145\t            reason=(\n146\t                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' has no ArgoCD mapping in config/clusters.yaml; \"\n147\t                f\"ArgoCD source omitted to avoid querying the wrong instance\"\n148\t            ),\n149\t        )\n150\t\n151\t    access = entry.get(\"access\")\n152\t    argocd_api = entry.get(\"argocd_api\")\n153\t\n154\t    if access == CONSUMABLE_ACCESS:\n155\t        if not argocd_api:\n156\t            return ArgocdResolution(\n157\t                cluster=cluster,\n158\t                argocd_api=None,\n159\t                access=access,\n160\t                satisfiable=False,\n161\t                reason=f\"Cluster '{cluster}' is read-only-proxy but defines no argocd_api\",\n162\t            )\n163\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n164\t            cluster=cluster,\n165\t            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n166\t            access=access,\n167\t            satisfiable=True,\n168\t        )\n169\t\n170\t    if access == \"authenticated\":\n171\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n172\t            cluster=cluster,\n173\t            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n174\t            access=access,\n175\t            satisfiable=False,\n176\t            reason=(\n177\t                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' ArgoCD requires authentication \"\n178\t                f\"(no no-auth read-only proxy available); ArgoCD source omitted\"\n179\t            ),\n180\t        )\n181\t\n182\t    return ArgocdResolution(\n183\t        cluster=cluster,\n184\t        argocd_api=argocd_api,\n185\t        access=access,\n186\t        satisfiable=False,\n187\t        reason=(\n188\t            f\"Cluster '{cluster}' uses unsupported ArgoCD access mode '{access}' \"\n189\t            f\"(fetch strand can only consume '{CONSUMABLE_ACCESS}')\"\n190\t        ),\n191\t    )\n192\t\n193\t\n194\tclass ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(Exception):\n195\t    \"\"\"Raised when an ArgoCD query cannot be issued without risking the wrong instance.\n196\t\n197\t    Carries the human-readable resolution ``reason`` so the orchestrator can\n198\t    surface it as a fetch_coverage caveat. Raising (rather than returning an\n199\t    error dict) integrates with the orchestrator's existing failure path: the\n200\t    source is bucketed as failed and a caveat is emitted, never a silent\n201\t    wrong-instance query.\n202\t    \"\"\"\n203\t\n204\t    def __init__(self, reason: str, cluster: Optional[str] = None):\n205\t        self.reason = reason\n206\t        self.cluster = cluster\n207\t        super().__init__(reason)\n208\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"161e2cae-4066-431a-9f1b-ca762db343c9","uuid":"4ea00ce8-9af5-42f6-b578-40d0fd3a14eb","timestamp":"2026-07-23T19:18:27.246Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/fetch/clusters.py","content":"\"\"\"\nCluster → ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh).\n\nThere is no single ArgoCD API: which instance holds a project's Application\ndepends on the cluster the project lives on. This module maps each cluster to\nits ArgoCD API base URL and an `access` mode, loaded from\n``config/clusters.yaml`` and hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache — the same\nstrategy every hot-reloaded config artifact uses (stat the file each call,\nre-read only when its mtime changed).\n\nThe fetch strand holds no ArgoCD credentials, so it can only *consume*\n``read-only-proxy`` endpoints. Any cluster that is unmapped, or mapped to an\naccess mode the strand cannot satisfy (``authenticated`` with no credential\npath), resolves as **unsatisfiable** — the ArgoCD source then fails with an\nhonest ``fetch_coverage`` caveat. This is what makes querying the wrong\ninstance impossible: a wrong-instance query would otherwise return not-found\n(indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\") and silently drop the sync row.\n\nSee docs/plan/plan.md → Fetch Strand → Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nimport yaml\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# config/clusters.yaml lives at the repo root under config/; this file is\n# src/fetch/clusters.py, so the repo root is three parents up.\nCLUSTERS_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / \"config\" / \"clusters.yaml\"\n\n# The only access mode the fetch strand can satisfy: no credentials needed,\n# a proxy injects a read-only token.\nCONSUMABLE_ACCESS = \"read-only-proxy\"\n\n# mtime-checked cache (mirrors src/components/hot_reload.py's pattern).\n_cache: Optional[dict] = None\n_cache_mtime: Optional[float] = None\n\n\ndef _read_clusters_file(path: Path = CLUSTERS_PATH) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Parse the raw YAML, returning the ``clusters:`` mapping (or {}).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text()) or {}\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"clusters config not found at {path}; all clusters unmapped\")\n        return {}\n    except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to parse clusters config {path}: {e}\")\n        # Preserve the previously-known-good cache rather than poisoning it:\n        # return whatever is cached (possibly empty) so a malformed edit does\n        # not flip every mapped cluster to unmapped mid-flight.\n        return _cache or {}\n    if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n        logger.error(f\"clusters config {path} is not a mapping (got {type(raw).__name__})\")\n        return _cache or {}\n    clusters = raw.get(\"clusters\", {})\n    if not isinstance(clusters, dict):\n        logger.error(f\"'clusters' in {path} is not a mapping (got {type(clusters).__name__})\")\n        return _cache or {}\n    return clusters\n\n\ndef get_clusters(force: bool = False) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the parsed ``clusters`` mapping, reloading only when the file's\n    mtime has changed (or when ``force`` is set, for tests).\n\n    A benign parse failure returns the last good cache instead of an empty\n    dict, so a transiently-malformed edit never silently unmaps everything.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n    try:\n        mtime = CLUSTERS_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n    except OSError:\n        # File vanished: keep the last-known-good cache (best effort).\n        return _cache or {}\n\n    if force or _cache is None or mtime != _cache_mtime:\n        parsed = _read_clusters_file()\n        # Don't poison the cache on a parse failure: _read_clusters_file\n        # already returns the prior cache in that case, so this is a no-op\n        # when parsing failed — but guard explicitly for clarity.\n        _cache = parsed\n        _cache_mtime = mtime\n        logger.debug(f\"Reloaded clusters config ({len(parsed)} clusters) from {CLUSTERS_PATH}\")\n    return _cache\n\n\ndef reset_cache() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Clear the mtime cache (test hook).\"\"\"\n    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n    _cache = None\n    _cache_mtime = None\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ArgocdResolution:\n    \"\"\"Result of resolving a cluster to an ArgoCD endpoint.\n\n    ``satisfiable`` is True only when the strand can actually issue the query —\n    i.e. the cluster is mapped AND its access mode is consumable without\n    credentials. When False, ``reason`` explains why (for the caveat strip).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    cluster: Optional[str]\n    argocd_api: Optional[str]\n    access: Optional[str]\n    satisfiable: bool\n    reason: Optional[str] = None\n\n\ndef resolve_argocd_endpoint(cluster: Optional[str]) -> ArgocdResolution:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a cluster name to a consumable ArgoCD API base URL.\n\n    Resolution rules (every unsatisfiable case carries a human-readable\n    ``reason`` for the fetch_coverage caveat):\n\n    * ``cluster`` is None/empty → unsatisfiable (\"no cluster configured …\")\n    * cluster absent from clusters.yaml → unsatisfiable (\"no ArgoCD mapping …\")\n    * access == ``read-only-proxy`` → satisfiable, argocd_api set\n    * access == ``authenticated`` → unsatisfiable (\"requires authentication …\")\n    * any other/unknown access mode → unsatisfiable (\"unsupported access mode …\")\n    \"\"\"\n    if not cluster:\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=None,\n            argocd_api=None,\n            access=None,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=\"No cluster configured for this project; ArgoCD endpoint cannot be resolved\",\n        )\n\n    entry = get_clusters().get(cluster)\n    if entry is None:\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=None,\n            access=None,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=(\n                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' has no ArgoCD mapping in config/clusters.yaml; \"\n                f\"ArgoCD source omitted to avoid querying the wrong instance\"\n            ),\n        )\n\n    access = entry.get(\"access\")\n    argocd_api = entry.get(\"argocd_api\")\n\n    if access == CONSUMABLE_ACCESS:\n        if not argocd_api:\n            return ArgocdResolution(\n                cluster=cluster,\n                argocd_api=None,\n                access=access,\n                satisfiable=False,\n                reason=f\"Cluster '{cluster}' is read-only-proxy but defines no argocd_api\",\n            )\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n            access=access,\n            satisfiable=True,\n        )\n\n    if access == \"authenticated\":\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n            access=access,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=(\n                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' ArgoCD requires authentication \"\n                f\"(no no-auth read-only proxy available); ArgoCD source omitted\"\n            ),\n        )\n\n    return ArgocdResolution(\n        cluster=cluster,\n        argocd_api=argocd_api,\n        access=access,\n        satisfiable=False,\n        reason=(\n            f\"Cluster '{cluster}' uses unsupported ArgoCD access mode '{access}' \"\n            f\"(fetch strand can only consume '{CONSUMABLE_ACCESS}')\"\n        ),\n    )\n\n\nclass ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when an ArgoCD query cannot be issued without risking the wrong instance.\n\n    Carries the human-readable resolution ``reason`` so the orchestrator can\n    surface it as a fetch_coverage caveat. Raising (rather than returning an\n    error dict) integrates with the orchestrator's existing failure path: the\n    source is bucketed as failed and a caveat is emitted, never a silent\n    wrong-instance query.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, reason: str, cluster: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.reason = reason\n        self.cluster = cluster\n        super().__init__(reason)\n","numLines":208,"startLine":1,"totalLines":208}}}

ensure fresh clusters.yaml read\n        client = mock_httpx(FakeResponse(json_data={\"items\": []}))\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # apexalgo-iad is mapped in clusters.yaml with access: authenticated\n        with pytest.raises(ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable) as exc_info:\n            await strand._fetch_argocd_app(\n                _ctx(app_name=\"test-app\", cluster=\"apexalgo-iad\")\n            )\n\n        # CRITICAL: no HTTP request was made — proving no wrong-instance query\n        assert client.requests == []\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unmapped_cluster_fails_fast_with_caveat(self, mock_httpx):\n        \"\"\"\n        A cluster absent from clusters.yaml (e.g., 'some-unmapped-cluster')\n        raises ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable with a reason mentioning no ArgoCD\n        mapping — ZERO HTTP requests issued. This proves a wrong-instance\n        query is impossible.\n        \"\"\"\n        from src.fetch.clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, reset_cache\n\n        reset_cache()  # ensure fresh clusters.yaml read\n        client = mock_httpx(FakeResponse(json_data={\"items\": []}))\n        strand = FetchStrand()\n\n        # src/fetch/orchestrator.py against a mocked HTTP layer —\nproving that each fetch source type (kubectl API, ArgoCD API, pod logs, events)\nparses its real response shape into the structured dict synthesize consumes.\n\nhttpx is faked, so no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, or live cluster is hit.\nNo subprocess or filesystem source is touched here (those are covered by the\nlocal-repo integration test at the end).\n\nWhat this suite locks down (the \"various fetch source types execute correctly\"\ncontract):\n\n1. **HTTP source types parse real payloads** — kubectl_pods, kubectl_deployments,\n   kubectl_workflows, argocd_app, logs, and events each turn a realistic API\n   response into the field set the component library expects.\n2. **Missing-argument guards** — every executor returns a structured error dict\n   (and makes no HTTP call) when its required context fields are absent, rather\n   than raising.\n3. **HTTP error handling** — 404s and empty-result envelopes become error dicts,\n   not exceptions (synthesize surfaces caveats, never crashes).\n4. **End-to-end strand wiring** — a real FetchStrand.fetch() over a STATUS\n   utterance, with httpx mocked and local sources stubbed, yields parsed data\n   for each HTTP source — i.e. the strand correctly maps source types to\n   executors to structured results.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock\n\nimport httpx\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.fetch import orchestrator\nfrom src.fetch.commands import FetchContext, FetchRequest, FetchSource, IntentType\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchStrand\n\n# --- httpx fakes -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FakeResponse:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for httpx.Response — serves canned JSON/text + status.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n        self._json_data = json_data\n        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n        self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n                response=self,\n            )\n\n\nclass FakeAsyncClient:\n    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, responder):\n        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n        self._responder = responder\n        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n        return False\n\n    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n        self.requests.append((url, params))\n        if callable(self._responder):\n            return self._responder(url, params)\n        return self._responder\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"\n    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n    that constructs one gets `responder`. Returns the installed client so tests\n    can assert on which URLs/params were hit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def install(responder) -> FakeAsyncClient:\n        client = FakeAsyncClient(responder)\n        # Executors construct via `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=..., verify=...)`;\n        # ignore those kwargs and hand back our instrumented client.\n        monkeypatch.setattr(orchestrator.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", lambda *a, **kw: client)\n        return client\n\n    return install\n\n\ndef _ctx(**kwargs) -> FetchContext:\n    \"\"\"FetchContext with sane kubectl-proxy defaults + per-test overrides.\"\"\"\n    defaults = {\n        \"proxy\": \"http://traefik-test:8001\",\n        \"namespace\": \"myns\",\n        \"project_slug\": \"my-proj\",\n    }\n    defaults.update(kwargs)\n    return FetchContext(**defaults)\n\n\n# --- 1. already handles these cases correctly:\n- `_fetch_argocd_app` calls `resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)` \n- If `resolution.satisfiable` is False, it raises `ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable` with the human-readable reason\n- This exception is raised BEFORE any HTTP call, preventing wrong-instance queries\n- The exception is caught by `_execute_source` and bucketed as a failed source\n- The fetch
